issues.body: OWL 2's property-hierarchy restriction explicitly permits every property-chain axiom whose superproperty is owl:topObjectProperty, before applying the transitivity/general/left-/right-identity cases. told_rbox_regular has no top-role input or exemption, so (for example) owl:topObjectProperty o owl:topObjectProperty o owl:topObjectProperty ⊑ owl:topObjectProperty returns false via the s < s check even though the normative restriction admits it. This makes Report::rbox_non_regular incorrectly true and contradicts its documented meaning. The focused tests cover the other special forms but not this normative case.
issues.fix_hint: Preserve the identity of owl:topObjectProperty through normalization (or exclude such told chains before this helper), unconditionally accept chains with that superproperty, and add both a helper-level test and an extraction/classify_graph test using a top-superproperty self-chain that currently false-positives. Keep the exception ahead of the self-constraint and cycle checks.
issues.title: Regularity checker omits the owl:topObjectProperty exemption

summary: The regularity flag is wired through the extraction-based APIs and the focused tests exercise the real classification path, but the checker omits a normative OWL 2 regularity exception. Property-chain axioms whose superproperty is owl:topObjectProperty are unconditionally admissible; the new algorithm can classify some of them as non-regular. Because the public Report field and documentation claim a true non-regularity verdict, this false positive is a correctness and honesty defect.
